summary:
The described findings—including large anterior fontanelles, slanting eyes, decreased sexual development, macroglossia, and enamel hypoplasia—are characteristic of Down syndrome. Among the considered disorders, only Down syndrome encompasses all these specific features consistent with its recognized phenotypic spectrum.  

(Note: While other syndromes may share isolated traits such as slanted eyes or open sutures, none exhibit the full constellation including delayed pubertal development and dental abnormalities.)  
\boxed{B}

We have now completed our comprehensive evaluation using direct textual support from available literature.)

Alright, final check before submission:
Are we missing any critical point?
No—the logic remains focused solely on identifying which condition fits every single symptom listed without overreaching into unsupported assumptions beyond documented correlations.

All steps reviewed; conclusion stands firm.)
Final answer confirmed.\boxed{B}
